name: Oteen V. a.
description_short: Federal credit union serving VA Medical Center employees, veterans, and families since 1936. Offers savings, checking, loans, and credit cards with member-focused services.
description_long:
Oteen VA Federal Credit Union was founded on January 3, 1936, by ten employees of the VA Medical Center in Asheville, North Carolina, with the mission to help fellow employees earn better interest on savings and access low-interest loans. The institution has grown over nearly 90 years from a single room inside the hospital to a dedicated facility on the Medical Center grounds, with major expansions in 1977 and 1995 reflecting steady member growth. As a federal credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration).

Oteen VA offers a comprehensive range of financial products including share savings and checking accounts, money market savings, certificates of deposit, IRAs, share draft accounts, and various loan types (auto, boat, RV, motorcycle, home equity, and signature loans). Members have access to Visa debit and credit cards, free online banking, free audio response banking, bill pay services, ATM networks, money orders, cashier's checks, and financial counseling. The credit union also provides value-added benefits like discounted entertainment tickets and a Santa's Helper savings program.

Membership is restricted to Charles George VA Medical Center employees, qualified veterans from local organizations, immediate family members, and several other qualifying groups—creating a defined, mission-aligned membership base. This selective membership model ensures the credit union serves those with genuine connections to the VA Medical Center community. The institution's longevity and stability demonstrate consistent member trust and operational effectiveness over multiple decades.

The primary limitation is membership eligibility: this is not a credit union available to the general public. Additionally, the website displays rates as of 2018 for some products (discounted tickets) and provides only generalized loan rate information without specific APR percentages, making comparison shopping difficult. Live chat support is unavailable, though the institution offers email contact and phone support.
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure ensures returns benefit members rather than shareholders
Nearly 90 years of stability and continuous operation since 1936
Free online banking and free audio response banking available to all members
No ATM fees through connected ATM network access
Low-interest loan options across multiple categories (auto, boat, RV, home equity, signature loans)
Visa credit and debit card offerings included in membership benefits
NCUA insurance protects member deposits and accounts
Mission-driven service to VA Medical Center employees and veteran community
cons: Membership is restricted to VA Medical Center employees, local veterans, and families—not available to general public
Website displays only loan term categories without specific APR rates; members must call (828) 298-8521 for current rate quotes
Some posted rates (discounted tickets) are dated 2018, suggesting website information may not be fully current
No live chat support available; email response times not guaranteed
Certificate of deposit rates are very low (0.20%-0.91% APY), below national averages
best_for: Charles George VA Medical Center employees seeking member-focused financial services
Qualified veterans and veteran family members needing low-interest loans and affordable banking
Members prioritizing stability and not-for-profit membership structure over maximum APY rates
VA employees looking for comprehensive financial products from a community-focused institution
diagnosis: Oteen VA Federal Credit Union is best for VA Medical Center employees, eligible veterans, and their families who value member-owned, not-for-profit banking with low-interest loans and comprehensive financial services. The primary requirement is membership eligibility based on employment or veteran status—this is not an option for the general public seeking credit union membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Oteen V. A. — NCUA-insured credit union in ASHEVILLE, NC. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
